blocks.content: The competition was Lena's idea, which should have been warning enough.
Galaxy Games ran a bi-monthly two-on-two bracket tournament — mostly regulars, mostly low-stakes, entirely loud — and Lena had entered them before Cassandra had any idea it was happening.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: I put you two down for the Thursday bracket.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You put Ethan down without asking him?

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: I put you both down. You said he was your person. I made a judgment call.
Ethan, who had just pushed through the door behind Cassandra, looked between them.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: What am I playing?

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Galaxy Games tournament. Thursday. You've apparently already been signed up.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: What game?

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: Mixed bracket. Racing, skee-ball, and one surprise round.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: What's the surprise round?

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: That's what makes it a surprise.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: That's not a reassuring answer.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: You'll be fine. Cassandra's competitive. She compensates for the unknown variables.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I compensate. I don't overprepare.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: Last time you made me do twenty practice rounds on skee-ball the day before because you said my release was inconsistent.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Your release was inconsistent.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: We came in second.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Out of three teams.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: Second is second.
She looked at Ethan with the appraising expression she reserved for people she was deciding about.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: For what it's worth — she gets a look when she's competitive. You'll recognize it when you see it.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I don't get a look.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: You absolutely get a look, Cass.
Cassandra turned to Ethan with the expression of someone who has made a decision.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: How are you at racing games?

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Adequate.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Adequate meaning you've played them, or adequate meaning you're genuinely good and being modest?

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Adequate meaning I've played them. I'm not exceptional.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Okay. We practice on Wednesday. I'll walk you through the form.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: You have a skee-ball form.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Everyone has a form. Most people just don't know what theirs is yet.
Lena said nothing. She watched Ethan look at Cassandra — the expression of someone who would have found a polite deflection for anyone else — and instead nod.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Wednesday.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Wednesday.
